item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was pretty clever! I was wondering where the article was going and it surprised me by taking it in a direction I never even considered. +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a synesthete myself, this is a fascinating concept. The build up from “it can make you experience what it’s like to be a synesthete“ to “everything reminds of of everything all at once” was a direction I wasn’t expecting but both greatly enjoyed and was greatly disturbed by! +1, and a sudden gratefulness for the fact that synesthesia doesn’t work like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Originally I didn’t have all those test logs there, and someone didn’t like that it went from nothing to the extreme so quickly. As a compromise I might leave out a few and clarify them more in the description, including the thing you were wondering about.

+ show block
– hide block

Edit: Also, to justify having logs there, I feel like it gives the reader context to what’s going on in the final part, and makes everything clear. You understand how they were going further and further with testing. You see how someone had a small chain reaction, hinting at what’s to come. And you see things becoming physical instead of just mental. Without this, I feel like the final incident would sort of come out of nowhere. They also make sure that the effect is clear, in case the description itself caused confusion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Man, I really want to like this. The idea of the chain reaction, taking a simple effect to its logical extreme, is really cool, and that final log is solidly written.

That said, this is way longer than it needs to be, pretty much all of the test logs in the middle could be tossed; they just give up boring details about the nitty gritty of how it works, none of which is relevant to the big emotional payoff. At the same time, several details that I thought should be clarified aren't. Are the associations permanent? How physical are the manifestations? Do they persist or wear off?

I waffled back and forth before deciding on a no-vote for this. The end was very good, but man it was a slog to get there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I’m so glad to hear you think so, thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have that same notebook or at least one that looks very similar to it~

Also I remember seeing this the first time you posted this, it has improved much from then!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you to RockTeethMothEyes, MaliceAforethought, Alces_alces, and people in chat (who I don't remember the names of, sorry) for critiquing this, as well as everyone who downvoted this to deletion on my first try. I believe this is much improved now, and I'm ready to improve it more if need be.

The image is mine.
